state.title: source_location

part.text:
Repo appears to be MapLibre Native (maplibre-gl-native). Key file: /app/src/mbgl/layout/symbol_instance.hpp has already an inline fallback for source_location? It includes <source_location> and has a conditional for __cplusplus <= 201703L or !__has_builtin(__builtin_source_location). It defines struct source_location in std namespace? That is UB (extending std). The user explicitly says "without extending std". The file likely defines `namespace std { struct source_location {...} }` which is illegal. Need fix that.

We need "OpenHarmony's C++ environment has no <source_location>. Give core a standards-safe fallback without extending std, and keep call sites useful for diagnostics. Support the normal standard-library path too, update relevant build manifests, and commit the finished change."

So our task: provide core with a standards-safe fallback for source_location. The current code in symbol_instance.hpp extends std with source_location. We need to create a core utility (maybe in include/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp) that uses std::source_location if available, else fallback. Call sites should remain useful for diagnostics: use a macro like MBGL_SOURCE_LOCATION_CURRENT default argument.

Need inspect symbol_instance.hpp and .cpp, bucket.hpp, symbol_bucket. Also need update build manifests. Build manifests: likely Bazel and CMake. The file is in src/mbgl/layout/symbol_instance.*, maybe bucket.hpp. Need check include paths.

Let's read relevant files.

43: // Mimic the PaintPropertyBinder technique specifically for the {text,icon}-size
44: // layout properties in order to provide a 'custom' scheme for encoding the
45: // necessary attribute data.  As with PaintPropertyBinder, SymbolSizeBinder is
46: // an abstract class whose implementations handle the particular attribute &
47: // uniform logic needed by each possible type of the {Text,Icon}Size properties.
48: class SymbolSizeBinder {
49: public:
50:     virtual ~SymbolSizeBinder() = default;
51: 
52:     static std::unique_ptr<SymbolSizeBinder> create(float tileZoom,
53:                                                     const style::PropertyValue<float>& sizeProperty,
54:                                                     float defaultValue);
55: 
56:     virtual Range<float> getVertexSizeData(const GeometryTileFeature& feature) = 0;
57:     virtual ZoomEvaluatedSize evaluateForZoom(float currentZoom) const = 0;
58: };

60: class ConstantSymbolSizeBinder final : public SymbolSizeBinder {
61: public:
62:     ConstantSymbolSizeBinder(const float /*tileZoom*/, const float& size, const float /*defaultValue*/) noexcept
63:         : layoutSize(size) {}
64: 
65:     ConstantSymbolSizeBinder(const float /*tileZoom*/, const style::Undefined&, const float defaultValue) noexcept
66:         : layoutSize(defaultValue) {}
67: 
68:     ConstantSymbolSizeBinder(const float tileZoom,
69:                              const style::PropertyExpression<float>& expression_,
70:                              const float /*defaultValue*/
71:                              )
72:         : layoutSize(expression_.evaluate(tileZoom + 1)),
73:           expression(expression_) {
74:         const Range<float> zoomLevels = expression_.getCoveringStops(tileZoom, tileZoom + 1);
75:         coveringRanges = std::make_tuple(
76:             zoomLevels, Range<float>{expression_.evaluate(zoomLevels.min), expression_.evaluate(zoomLevels.max)});
77:     }
78: 
79:     Range<float> getVertexSizeData(const GeometryTileFeature&) noexcept override { return {0.0f, 0.0f}; };
80: 
81:     ZoomEvaluatedSize evaluateForZoom(float currentZoom) const override {
82:         float size = layoutSize;
83:         bool isZoomConstant = !(coveringRanges || expression);
84:         if (coveringRanges) {
85:             // Even though we could get the exact value of the camera function
86:             // at z = currentZoom, we intentionally do not: instead, we
87:             // interpolate between the camera function values at a pair of zoom
88:             // stops covering [tileZoom, tileZoom + 1] in order to be consistent
89:             // with this restriction on composite functions.
90:             const Range<float>& zoomLevels = std::get<0>(*coveringRanges);
91:             const Range<float>& sizeLevels = std::get<1>(*coveringRanges);
92:             float t = util::clamp(expression->interpolationFactor(zoomLevels, currentZoom), 0.0f, 1.0f);
93:             size = sizeLevels.min + t * (sizeLevels.max - sizeLevels.min);
94:         } else if (expression) {
95:             size = expression->evaluate(currentZoom);
96:         }
97: 
98:         const float unused = 0.0f;
99:         return {.isZoomConstant = isZoomConstant,
100:                 .isFeatureConstant = true,
101:                 .sizeT = unused,
102:                 .size = size,
103:                 .layoutSize = layoutSize};
104:     }
105: 
106:     float layoutSize;
107:     std::optional<std::tuple<Range<float>, Range<float>>> coveringRanges;
108:     std::optional<style::PropertyExpression<float>> expression;
109: };

116:         : expression(std::move(expression_)),
117:           defaultValue(defaultValue_) {}
118: 
119:     Range<float> getVertexSizeData(const GeometryTileFeature& feature) override {
120:         const float size = expression.evaluate(feature, defaultValue);
121:         return {size, size};
122:     };
123: 
124:     ZoomEvaluatedSize evaluateForZoom(float) const noexcept override {
125:         const float unused = 0.0f;
126:         return {
127:             .isZoomConstant = true, .isFeatureConstant = false, .sizeT = unused, .size = unused, .layoutSize = unused};
128:     }

136:     CompositeFunctionSymbolSizeBinder(const float tileZoom,
137:                                       style::PropertyExpression<float> expression_,
138:                                       const float defaultValue_) noexcept
139:         : expression(std::move(expression_)),
140:           defaultValue(defaultValue_),
141:           layoutZoom(tileZoom + 1),
142:           coveringZoomStops(expression.getCoveringStops(tileZoom, tileZoom + 1)) {}
143: 
144:     Range<float> getVertexSizeData(const GeometryTileFeature& feature) override {
145:         return {expression.evaluate(coveringZoomStops.min, feature, defaultValue),
146:                 expression.evaluate(coveringZoomStops.max, feature, defaultValue)};
147:     };
148: 
149:     ZoomEvaluatedSize evaluateForZoom(float currentZoom) const override {
150:         float sizeInterpolationT = util::clamp(
151:             expression.interpolationFactor(coveringZoomStops, currentZoom), 0.0f, 1.0f);
152: 
153:         const float unused = 0.0f;
154:         return {.isZoomConstant = false,
155:                 .isFeatureConstant = false,
156:                 .sizeT = sizeInterpolationT,
157:                 .size = unused,
158:                 .layoutSize = unused};
159:     }

19: std::unique_ptr<SymbolSizeBinder> SymbolSizeBinder::create(const float tileZoom,
20:                                                            const style::PropertyValue<float>& sizeProperty,
21:                                                            const float defaultValue) {
22:     return sizeProperty.match(
23:         [&](const Undefined& value) -> std::unique_ptr<SymbolSizeBinder> {
24:             return std::make_unique<ConstantSymbolSizeBinder>(tileZoom, value, defaultValue);
25:         },
26:         [&](float value) -> std::unique_ptr<SymbolSizeBinder> {
27:             return std::make_unique<ConstantSymbolSizeBinder>(tileZoom, value, defaultValue);
28:         },
29:         [&](const style::PropertyExpression<float>& expression) -> std::unique_ptr<SymbolSizeBinder> {
30:             if (expression.isFeatureConstant()) {
31:                 return std::make_unique<ConstantSymbolSizeBinder>(tileZoom, expression, defaultValue);
32:             } else if (expression.isZoomConstant()) {
33:                 return std::make_unique<SourceFunctionSymbolSizeBinder>(tileZoom, expression, defaultValue);
34:             } else {
35:                 return std::make_unique<CompositeFunctionSymbolSizeBinder>(tileZoom, expression, defaultValue);
36:             }
37:         });
38: }

115: bool SymbolBucket::hasIconCollisionCircleData() const {
116:     return iconCollisionCircle && !iconCollisionCircle->segments.empty();
117: }
118: 
119: bool SymbolBucket::hasTextCollisionBoxData() const {
120:     return textCollisionBox && !textCollisionBox->segments.empty();
121: }

124:     return textCollisionCircle && !textCollisionCircle->segments.empty();

127: void addPlacedSymbol(gfx::IndexVector<gfx::Triangles>& triangles, const PlacedSymbol& placedSymbol) {
128:     auto endIndex = placedSymbol.vertexStartIndex + placedSymbol.glyphOffsets.size() * 4;
129:     for (auto vertexIndex = placedSymbol.vertexStartIndex; vertexIndex < endIndex; vertexIndex += 4) {
130:         triangles.emplace_back(static_cast<uint16_t>(vertexIndex + 0),
131:                                static_cast<uint16_t>(vertexIndex + 1),
132:                                static_cast<uint16_t>(vertexIndex + 2));
133:         triangles.emplace_back(static_cast<uint16_t>(vertexIndex + 1),
134:                                static_cast<uint16_t>(vertexIndex + 2),
135:                                static_cast<uint16_t>(vertexIndex + 3));
136:     }
137: }
138: 
139: void SymbolBucket::sortFeatures(const float angle) {
140:     if (!sortFeaturesByY) {
141:         return;
142:     }
143:     assert(angle != std::numeric_limits<float>::max());
144: 
145:     if (sortedAngle == angle) {
146:         return;
147:     }
148: 
149:     sortedAngle = angle;
150: 
151:     // The current approach to sorting doesn't sort across text and icon
152:     // segments so don't try. Sorting within segments separately seemed not to
153:     // be worth the complexity.
154:     if (text.segments.size() > 1 || (icon.segments.size() > 1 || sdfIcon.segments.size() > 1)) {
155:         return;
156:     }
157: 
158:     sortUploaded = false;
159:     uploaded = false;
160: 
161:     text.triangles.clear();
162:     icon.triangles.clear();
163:     sdfIcon.triangles.clear();
164: 
165:     auto symbolsSortOrder = std::make_unique<std::vector<size_t>>();
166:     symbolsSortOrder->reserve(symbolInstances.size());
167: 
168:     // If the symbols are allowed to overlap sort them by their vertical screen
169:     // position. The index array buffer is rewritten to reference the
170:     // (unchanged) vertices in the sorted order.
171:     for (const SymbolInstance& symbolInstance : getSortedSymbols(angle)) {
172:         if (!symbolInstance.check(SYM_GUARD_LOC) ||
173:             !symbolInstance.checkIndexes(
174:                 text.placedSymbols.size(), icon.placedSymbols.size(), sdfIcon.placedSymbols.size(), SYM_GUARD_LOC)) {
175:             continue;
176:         }
177:         symbolsSortOrder->push_back(symbolInstance.getDataFeatureIndex());
178: 
179:         if (symbolInstance.getPlacedRightTextIndex()) {
180:             addPlacedSymbol(text.triangles, text.placedSymbols[*symbolInstance.getPlacedRightTextIndex()]);
181:         }
182: 
183:         if (symbolInstance.getPlacedCenterTextIndex() && !symbolInstance.getSingleLine()) {
184:             addPlacedSymbol(text.triangles, text.placedSymbols[*symbolInstance.getPlacedCenterTextIndex()]);
185:         }
186: 
187:         if (symbolInstance.getPlacedLeftTextIndex() && !symbolInstance.getSingleLine()) {
188:             addPlacedSymbol(text.triangles, text.placedSymbols[*symbolInstance.getPlacedLeftTextIndex()]);
189:         }
190: 
191:         if (symbolInstance.getPlacedVerticalTextIndex()) {
192:             addPlacedSymbol(text.triangles, text.placedSymbols[*symbolInstance.getPlacedVerticalTextIndex()]);
193:         }
194: 
195:         auto& iconBuffer = symbolInstance.hasSdfIcon() ? sdfIcon : icon;
196:         if (symbolInstance.getPlacedIconIndex()) {
197:             addPlacedSymbol(iconBuffer.triangles, iconBuffer.placedSymbols[*symbolInstance.getPlacedIconIndex()]);
198:         }
199: 
200:         if (symbolInstance.getPlacedVerticalIconIndex()) {
201:             addPlacedSymbol(iconBuffer.triangles,
202:                             iconBuffer.placedSymbols[*symbolInstance.getPlacedVerticalIconIndex()]);
203:         }
204:     }
205: 
206:     featureSortOrder = std::move(symbolsSortOrder);
207: }
208: 
209: SymbolInstanceReferences SymbolBucket::getSortedSymbols(const float angle) const {
210:     SymbolInstanceReferences result(symbolInstances.begin(), symbolInstances.end());
211:     const float sin = std::sin(angle);
212:     const float cos = std::cos(angle);
213: 
214:     std::ranges::sort(result, [sin, cos](const SymbolInstance& a, const SymbolInstance& b) {
215:         const auto aRotated = std::lround(sin * a.getAnchor().point.x + cos * a.getAnchor().point.y);
216:         const auto bRotated = std::lround(sin * b.getAnchor().point.x + cos * b.getAnchor().point.y);
217:         if (aRotated != bRotated) {
218:             return aRotated < bRotated;
219:         }
220:         return a.getDataFeatureIndex() > b.getDataFeatureIndex(); // aRotated == bRotated
221:     });
222: 
223:     return result;
224: }
225: 
226: SymbolInstanceReferences SymbolBucket::getSymbols(const std::optional<SortKeyRange>& range) const {
227:     assert(!range || range->start < range->end);
228:     assert(!range || range->end <= symbolInstances.size());
229:     if (!range || range->start >= range->end || range->end > symbolInstances.size()) {
230:         return {symbolInstances.begin(), symbolInstances.end()};
231:     }
232:     using offset_t = decltype(symbolInstances)::difference_type;
233:     return {symbolInstances.begin() + static_cast<offset_t>(range->start),
234:             symbolInstances.begin() + static_cast<offset_t>(range->end)};
235: }
236: 
237: #if MLN_SYMBOL_GUARDS
238: bool SymbolBucket::check(std::source_location source) {
239:     if (text.vertices().elements() != text.dynamicVertices().elements() ||
240:         text.vertices().elements() != text.opacityVertices().elements() ||
241:         icon.vertices().elements() != icon.dynamicVertices().elements() ||
242:         icon.vertices().elements() != icon.opacityVertices().elements() ||
243:         sdfIcon.vertices().elements() != sdfIcon.dynamicVertices().elements() ||
244:         sdfIcon.vertices().elements() != sdfIcon.opacityVertices().elements()) {
245:         // This bucket was left in a partial state and it cannot be used
246:         return false;
247:     }
248: 
249:     for (std::size_t i = 0; i < symbolInstances.size(); ++i) {
250:         if (!symbolInstances[i].check(source)) {
251:             return false;
252:         }
253:     }
254:     return true;
255: }
256: #endif
257: 
258: bool SymbolBucket::hasFormatSectionOverrides() const {
259:     if (!hasFormatSectionOverrides_) {
260:         hasFormatSectionOverrides_ = SymbolLayerPaintPropertyOverrides::hasOverrides(layout->get<TextField>());
261:     }
262:     return *hasFormatSectionOverrides_;
263: }
264: 
265: bool SymbolBucket::hasVariableTextAnchors() const {
266:     auto hasTextVariableAnchorOffset = [&]() -> bool {
267:         auto tvao = layout->get<TextVariableAnchorOffset>();
268:         if (tvao.isConstant()) {
269:             const auto constValue = tvao.constant();
270:             return constValue && !constValue->empty();
271:         } else if (tvao.isExpression()) {
272:             return true;
273:         }
274: 
275:         return false;
276:     };
277: 
278:     return hasTextVariableAnchorOffset() || !layout->get<TextVariableAnchor>().empty();
279: }

281: std::pair<uint32_t, bool> SymbolBucket::registerAtCrossTileIndex(CrossTileSymbolLayerIndex& index,
282:                                                                  const RenderTile& renderTile) {
283:     bool firstTimeAdded = index.addBucket(renderTile.getOverscaledTileID(), renderTile.matrix, *this);
284:     return std::make_pair(bucketInstanceId, firstTimeAdded);
285: }
286: 
287: void SymbolBucket::place(Placement& placement, const BucketPlacementData& data, std::set<uint32_t>& seenIds) {
288:     placement.placeSymbolBucket(data, seenIds);
289: }
290: 
291: void SymbolBucket::updateVertices(const Placement& placement,
292:                                   bool updateOpacities,
293:                                   const TransformState& state,
294:                                   const RenderTile& tile,
295:                                   std::set<uint32_t>& seenIds) {
296:     if (updateOpacities) {
297:         placement.updateBucketOpacities(*this, state, seenIds);
298:         placementChangesUploaded = false;
299:         uploaded = false;
300:     }
301: 
302:     if (placement.updateBucketDynamicVertices(*this, state, tile)) {
303:         dynamicUploaded = false;
304:         uploaded = false;
305:     }
306: 
307:     if (!uploaded) {
308:         text.updateModified();
309:         icon.updateModified();
310:         sdfIcon.updateModified();
311:     }
312: }

display.text:
load("@aspect_rules_js//js:defs.bzl", "js_binary", "js_library", "js_run_binary")
load("@bazel_skylib//rules:common_settings.bzl", "bool_flag", "string_flag")
load("@npm//:defs.bzl", "npm_link_all_packages")
load("@rules_cc//cc:defs.bzl", "cc_library")
load(
    "//bazel:core.bzl",
    "MLN_CORE_HEADERS",
    "MLN_CORE_SOURCE",
    "MLN_DRAWABLES_GL_HEADERS",
    "MLN_DRAWABLES_GL_SOURCE",
    "MLN_DRAWABLES_HEADERS",
    "MLN_DRAWABLES_MTL_HEADERS",
    "MLN_DRAWABLES_MTL_SOURCE",
    "MLN_DRAWABLES_SOURCE",
    "MLN_DRAWABLES_WEBGPU_HEADERS",
    "MLN_DRAWABLES_WEBGPU_SOURCE",
    "MLN_GENERATED_OPENGL_SHADER_HEADERS",
    "MLN_GENERATED_SHADER_HEADERS",
    "MLN_GENERATED_SHADER_SOURCE",
    "MLN_GENERATED_STYLE_SOURCE",
    "MLN_LAYER_PLUGIN_HEADERS",
    "MLN_LAYER_PLUGIN_SOURCE",
    "MLN_OPENGL_HEADERS",
    "MLN_OPENGL_SOURCE",
    "MLN_PRIVATE_GENERATED_STYLE_HEADERS",
    "MLN_PUBLIC_GENERATED_STYLE_HEADERS",
    "MLN_SHAPING_HARFBUZZ_SRCS",
)
load("//bazel:flags.bzl", "CPP_FLAGS", "MAPLIBRE_FLAGS")

# Generate code required by the core

js_run_binary(
    name = "generated_style_code",
    srcs = glob(
        ["**/*.ejs"],
        allow_empty = False,
    ),
    outs = MLN_PUBLIC_GENERATED_STYLE_HEADERS +
           MLN_PRIVATE_GENERATED_STYLE_HEADERS +
           MLN_GENERATED_STYLE_SOURCE,
    tool = ":generate-style-code-script",
)

js_run_binary(
    name = "generated_shaders",
    srcs = ["shaders/manifest.json"] + glob(
        ["shaders/*.glsl"],
        allow_empty = False,
    ),
    outs = MLN_GENERATED_SHADER_HEADERS +
           MLN_GENERATED_SHADER_SOURCE +
           MLN_GENERATED_OPENGL_SHADER_HEADERS,
    tool = ":generate-shader-code-script",
)

# This header only target strips the __generated__ prefix for the compiler
# search paths, making the location of generated code transparent to the build
cc_library(
    name = "mbgl-core-generated-public-artifacts",
    hdrs = [
               ":generated_shaders",
               ":generated_style_code",
           ] + MLN_PUBLIC_GENERATED_STYLE_HEADERS +
           MLN_GENERATED_SHADER_HEADERS +
           select({
               "//:metal_renderer": [],
               "//conditions:default": MLN_GENERATED_OPENGL_SHADER_HEADERS,
           }),
    visibility = ["//visibility:public"],
)

# ditto, but for private headers (under the src/ path)
cc_library(
    name = "mbgl-core-generated-private-artifacts",
    hdrs = [":generated_style_code"] + MLN_PRIVATE_GENERATED_STYLE_HEADERS,
    visibility = ["//visibility:private"],
)

# Generated source is inserted directly into the core target, no need to remove
# the prefix from the path.
cc_library(
    name = "mbgl-core",
    srcs = MLN_LAYER_PLUGIN_SOURCE +
           MLN_CORE_SOURCE +
           MLN_GENERATED_SHADER_SOURCE +
           MLN_GENERATED_STYLE_SOURCE + select({
        ":drawable_renderer": MLN_OPENGL_SOURCE + MLN_DRAWABLES_SOURCE + MLN_DRAWABLES_GL_SOURCE,
        ":metal_renderer": MLN_DRAWABLES_SOURCE + MLN_DRAWABLES_MTL_SOURCE,
        ":webgpu_renderer": MLN_DRAWABLES_SOURCE + MLN_DRAWABLES_WEBGPU_SOURCE,
        "//conditions:default": [],
    }) + select({
        "//:harfbuzz_text_shaping": MLN_SHAPING_HARFBUZZ_SRCS,
        "//conditions:default": [],
    }),
    hdrs = MLN_LAYER_PLUGIN_HEADERS + MLN_CORE_HEADERS + select({
        ":drawable_renderer": MLN_OPENGL_HEADERS + MLN_DRAWABLES_HEADERS + MLN_DRAWABLES_GL_HEADERS,
        ":metal_renderer": MLN_DRAWABLES_HEADERS + MLN_DRAWABLES_MTL_HEADERS,
        ":webgpu_renderer": MLN_DRAWABLES_HEADERS + MLN_DRAWABLES_WEBGPU_HEADERS,
        "//conditions:default": [],
    }),
    copts = CPP_FLAGS + MAPLIBRE_FLAGS,
    defines = select({
        ":drawable_renderer": [
            "MLN_RENDER_BACKEND_OPENGL=1",
        ],
        ":metal_renderer": [
            "MLN_RENDER_BACKEND_METAL=1",
        ],
        ":webgpu_renderer": [
            "MLN_RENDER_BACKEND_WEBGPU=1",
        ],
    }) + select({
        "@platforms//os:ios": ["GLES_SILENCE_DEPRECATION=1"],
        "//conditions:default": [],
    }) + select({
        "//:harfbuzz_text_shaping": ["MLN_TEXT_SHAPING_HARFBUZZ=1"],
        "//conditions:default": [],
    }),
    includes = [
        "include",
        "src",
    ] + select({
        ":metal_renderer": [
            "vendor/metal-cpp",
        ],
        "//conditions:default": [],
    }) + select({
        "//:harfbuzz_text_shaping": [
            "vendor/freetype/include",
            "vendor/harfbuzz/src",
        ],
        "//conditions:default": [],
    }),
    local_defines = [
        r"MLN_VERSION_REV=\"standalone\"",
    ],
    visibility = ["//visibility:public"],
    deps = [
        ":mbgl-core-generated-private-artifacts",
        ":mbgl-core-generated-public-artifacts",
        "//vendor:boost",
        "//vendor:earcut.hpp",
        "//vendor:eternal",
        "//vendor:expected-lite",
        "//vendor:maplibre-native-base",
        "//vendor:parsedate",
        "//vendor:pmtiles",
        "//vendor:polylabel",
        "//vendor:protozero",
        "//vendor:rapidjson",
        "//vendor:supercluster.hpp",
        "//vendor:unique_resource",
        "//vendor:unordered_dense",
        "//vendor:vector-tile",
        "//vendor:wagyu",
        "//vendor/maplibre-tile-spec/cpp:mlt_cpp",
    ] + select({
        "@platforms//os:ios": [
            "//vendor:ghc_filesystem",
            "//vendor:icu",
        ],
        "@platforms//os:osx": [
            "//vendor:icu",
        ],
        "@platforms//os:linux": [
            "//vendor:sqlite",
        ],
        "//conditions:default": [],
    }) + select({
        ":metal_renderer": [
            "//vendor:metal-cpp",
        ],
        "//conditions:default": [],
    }) + select({
        ":harfbuzz_text_shaping": [
            "//vendor:freetype",
            "//vendor:harfbuzz",
        ],
        "//conditions:default": [],
    }) + select({
        ":rust": [
            "//rustutils:rustutilslib",
        ],
        "//conditions:default": [
            "//vendor:csscolorparser",
        ],
    }),
)

# Selects the rendering implementation to utilize in the core

string_flag(
    name = "renderer",
    build_setting_default = "drawable",
    values = [
        "drawable",
        "metal",
        "webgpu",
    ],
)

config_setting(
    name = "drawable_renderer",
    flag_values = {
        ":renderer": "drawable",
    },
)

config_setting(
    name = "metal_renderer",
    flag_values = {
        ":renderer": "metal",
    },
)

config_setting(
    name = "webgpu_renderer",
    flag_values = {
        ":renderer": "webgpu",
    },
)

# Selects the shaping implementation to utilize in the core

string_flag(
    name = "shaping",
    build_setting_default = "harfbuzz",
    values = [
        "legacy",
        "harfbuzz",
    ],
)

config_setting(
    name = "harfbuzz_text_shaping",
    flag_values = {
        ":shaping": "harfbuzz",
    },
)

config_setting(
    name = "legacy_text_shaping",
    flag_values = {
        ":shaping": "legacy",
    },
)

bool_flag(
    name = "use_rust",
    build_setting_default = False,
    visibility = ["//visibility:public"],
)

config_setting(
    name = "rust",
    flag_values = {
        "//:use_rust": "true",
    },
)

exports_files(
    [
        "LICENSE.md",
        "scripts/style-spec-reference/v8.json",
    ],
    visibility = ["//visibility:public"],
)

npm_link_all_packages(
    name = "node_modules",
)

js_binary(
    name = "generate-style-code-script",
    data = [
        ":node_modules/csscolorparser",
        ":style-code",
        ":style-spec",
    ],
    entry_point = "scripts/generate-style-code.mjs",
)

js_binary(
    name = "generate-shader-code-script",
    entry_point = "shaders/generate_shader_code.mjs",
)

js_library(
    name = "style-spec",
    srcs = ["scripts/style-spec.mjs"],
    data = glob([
        "scripts/style-spec-reference/*.json",
    ]),
    visibility = ["//visibility:public"],
)

js_library(
    name = "style-code",
    srcs = ["scripts/style-code.mjs"],
    visibility = ["//visibility:public"],
    deps = [
        ":node_modules/ejs",
    ],
)

cc_library(
    name = "maplibre_lib",
    srcs = [],
    visibility = ["//visibility:public"],
    deps = select({
        "@platforms//os:macos": ["//platform:macos-objcpp"],
        "//conditions:default": [],
    }),
)

part.text: Bazel MLN_CORE_SOURCE includes both .cpp and .hpp (no separate headers list). We need add our new header in MLN_CORE_SOURCE (since it is in src). If we create include/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp, it would be MLN_CORE_HEADERS. But to keep internal and used in src, maybe put in include/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp to align with public headers. Or in src/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp. Since it's used by src internal headers and also maybe symbol_instance.hpp. The existing code includes src/mbgl/util headers. Let's look at an example mbgl util header.

part.text:
Notably this file also extends std for Android polyfill (legal? adding function templates). But our task specifically about source_location. We need not touch unrelated.

Need inspect tests or other uses. The check signatures are in public-ish headers? symbol_instance.hpp is src internal. bucket.hpp is internal. The new utility could be src internal. We can place it in `src/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp` and `src/mbgl/util/source_location.cpp`. But it must be included by symbol_instance.hpp and bucket.hpp. Since those are src/mbgl/..., they can include `mbgl/util/source_location.hpp` (which is in include? Actually if src/mbgl/util/... has same path as include? There are headers in src/mbgl/util too). For include with angle brackets `mbgl/util/...` Bazel includes src and include directories, so both work. Need add to both Bazel and CMake. If in src/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp, we add to MLN_CORE_SOURCE. If in include/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp, add to MLN_CORE_HEADERS. Which is better? Since used internally in src headers and not part of public API, maybe src/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp. But the user says "core" - maybe there is a `mbgl/util` core utilities. Let's search existing util header. Maybe `src/mbgl/util/string.hpp` or `include/mbgl/util/string.hpp`. Most util are in include. The source_location utility should likely be in include/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp because it's a generic utility and we need to include from src/mbgl/layout and src/mbgl/renderer. Many util headers are public. But also source_location.hpp might be in include/mbgl/util/std.hpp (a polyfill). Let's look at std.hpp.

    std::uint_least32_t line_;
    std::uint_least32_t column_;

                             std::uint_least32_t line,
                             std::uint_least32_t column = 0) noexcept

    constexpr std::uint_least32_t line() const noexcept { return line_; }

        const char* function = __builtin_FUNCTION(),
        std::uint_least32_t line = __builtin_LINE(),
        std::uint_least32_t column = __builtin_COLUMN()) noexcept {

    }
#endif
};
#endif

part.text:
Now we need to verify. The `source_location.hpp` uses `__func__` in a macro. But `__func__` is a local variable, so in a macro used in a function argument, fine. However, if `MBGL_SOURCE_LOCATION_CURRENT()` is used at namespace scope or in a default argument, it would fail. But it's only used in function calls.

Now, test compile. Let's see if we can compile the relevant files. We need to check build environment. We can use `cmake --build`? There might be a build directory. Let's check.

clang-format removed the indentation of preprocessor directives. The project style seems to not indent preprocessor. That's fine.

Now, after formatting, we need rebuild? The formatting changes only the header but not semantics. However, it may not affect compiled code. We should still rebuild to ensure no error. But it's already built. Since only formatting changed, the preprocessed output same. But to be safe, we can touch and rebuild? It will recompile only files including changed header? Many files include symbol_instance.hpp, which includes source_location.hpp. But formatting changed source_location.hpp only, not symbol_instance.hpp. The build may detect timestamp and recompile dependent files. We can just run the build again quickly.

part.text:
Diff looks good. The `SYM_GUARD_LOC` macro in disabled mode got formatted as `\n    {}` with backslash. That's fine.

Now, one thing: We should double-check the `__func__` usage in the macro. The `clang-format` made `SYM_GUARD_LOC` macro in disabled mode:

```
#define SYM_GUARD_LOC \
    {}
```

That's fine. But in the `MBGL_SOURCE_LOCATION_CURRENT()` macro, it uses `__func__`. Good.

Now, is there any issue with `__func__` in a `constexpr` constructor? The fallback `SourceLocation` constructor is `constexpr`. The macro creates a temporary with `__FILE__`, `__func__`, `__LINE__`. `__func__` is a const char array, fine. `__LINE__` is integer literal. `__FILE__` is string literal. This is a constant expression? `__func__` is not a string literal but a variable `const char __func__[] = "function-name";` In a `constexpr` constructor, the argument `functionName` is a `const char*` pointing to an array. The constructor is `constexpr` and copies the pointer. That's allowed as a constant expression? In C++, a constexpr constructor can have pointer parameters pointing to objects with static storage duration; `__func__` is a local array in the function, but a temporary `SourceLocation` object created at runtime? Actually `__func__` is a local variable; its address is not a constant expression? But `SourceLocation` constructor is `constexpr` and we are creating a temporary with a pointer to a local variable. In C++17, constant expression evaluation cannot read the value of a local variable? Wait, the constructor just stores the pointer, not dereferences it. The temporary object is not a constant expression if it contains a pointer to a local variable? Actually `constexpr` functions can take pointers, but the object itself is not a constant expression because it contains a pointer with automatic storage. But we don't need it to be a constant expression. The constructor is `constexpr` for when it is used with constant strings (e.g., `SourceLocation("", "", 0)`). The macro creates a temporary in a function call, which is not constant expression. That's fine.

But `std::source_location::current()` is `consteval`, so the macro in standard path creates a constant expression. The fallback type is `constexpr` constructor, not `consteval`. The macro expands to `::mbgl::SourceLocation(__FILE__, __func__, __LINE__)`. Is this a constant expression? It is a prvalue temporary with a pointer to local variable `__func__`. That is not a constant expression because the pointer is not a constant expression? Actually the `__func__` array is not a constant expression object, but its address is not a constant expression? In C++14, the address of an object is not a constant expression unless static. `constexpr` constructor can accept non-constant arguments and return a non-constant object. But the constructor itself is constexpr. The temporary may be evaluated at runtime. That's fine.

However, there might be an issue: `constexpr` constructor is called with a runtime `const char*`; the compiler can still evaluate it at compile time if the arguments are constants. But not required. The `SYM_GUARD_LOC` is used as a function argument; no requirement for constant expression.

But `SourceLocation::current()` in fallback uses default args with `__builtin_FILE()` etc. Those functions are builtins that return constant strings? They might be evaluated at call site. The function is `static constexpr` and returns an object. Again, the returned object contains a pointer to `__builtin_FILE()` string which is constant. That's fine.

Potential issue: The `__builtin_FUNCTION()` and `__builtin_FILE()` default arguments are supported by Clang and GCC, but not MSVC. We guard with `__has_builtin`. Good.

Now, also consider that the standard path `MBGL_SOURCE_LOCATION_CURRENT()` uses `::mbgl::SourceLocation::current()` which is `consteval` for `std::source_location`. The macro expands to a call. In C++20, `std::source_location::current()` is a `consteval` static member function. It can be used as an argument. Good.

Now, the disabled macro: `SYM_GUARD_LOC {}`. In call sites, when MLN_SYMBOL_GUARDS disabled, the check function signatures take `std::string_view = {}`. Passing `{}` works. But if `SYM_GUARD_LOC` is used as a default argument in some function signature? No. Good.

Now, check if there are any other build manifests, e.g., for Android or iOS. Search for `source_location` or `symbol_instance` in `platform/`.

part.text:
Interesting: In C++17 mode, `__builtin_COLUMN()` is not available in GCC? The compiler is GCC? It says not declared. Actually `__builtin_COLUMN` is a Clang extension; GCC doesn't have it. We guard `current()` with `__has_builtin(__builtin_FILE)` but not `__builtin_COLUMN`. If `__builtin_FILE` is available but `__builtin_COLUMN` isn't, compile fails. We need guard the current() function with all builtins or provide fallback. Since we don't use current() in macro, but it's defined. The fallback uses `__builtin_COLUMN()` in default arg. We need to guard current() with `__has_builtin(__builtin_COLUMN)` too, or use `__builtin_LINE()` for column? Actually `__builtin_COLUMN` is optional. We can guard current() with `__has_builtin(__builtin_FILE) && __has_builtin(__builtin_FUNCTION) && __has_builtin(__builtin_LINE) && __has_builtin(__builtin_COLUMN)`. If any missing, don't define current(). The macro doesn't use current(), so still works.

Let's update header. Also test C++20 passed because the standard path alias is used and current() not compiled. Good.

Let's fix the guard.

state.title: cmake --build /app/build-linux-opengl --target mbgl-core -j$(nproc)

part.text:
Build succeeded. Good.

Now, we need to decide whether to run the test runner. It might not be built. Building it would compile test files. Could take time. But our change is compile-time and core builds. However, the test runner includes the same headers. If we build it, it would further verify. But maybe too time-consuming. Let's consider building `mbgl-test-runner` target. It might take a while but only test files. The user requested "verify build and run tests". Since they specifically asked to commit finished change, maybe running tests is good. But we can run a subset or just build the test runner. Let's see if the build system has target `mbgl-test-runner`. The ctest expects it. Let's try building it. It may compile many test files. But the core is already built; tests compile test files. Could take a few minutes. Let's try with timeout.

part.text:
Now respond to user. Need summarize what was done, mention verification. Keep concise.

Need mention: introduced `src/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp` with `mbgl::SourceLocation` alias to `std::source_location` when available; fallback struct with same interface and `MBGL_SOURCE_LOCATION_CURRENT()` macro; removed the `namespace std` shim in `symbol_instance.hpp`; updated call sites and signatures to use `mbgl::SourceLocation`; updated `CMakeLists.txt` and `bazel/core.bzl`; verified `mbgl-core` and `mbgl-test-runner` build; note runtime tests could not run due to no EGL/display. Committed.

We should also mention commit hash.

Done. I added a standards-safe `source_location` abstraction in core and committed it.

**What changed**
- New `src/mbgl/util/source_location.hpp`:
  - `mbgl::SourceLocation` is a type alias to `std::source_location` when `<source_location>` and the C++20 feature are available.
  - Otherwise it falls back to a small `constexpr` struct with the same `file_name()`, `function_name()`, `line()`, `column()` interface.
  - Provides `MBGL_SOURCE_LOCATION_CURRENT()` so call sites keep useful diagnostics.
- Removed the old `namespace std { struct source_location { ... } }` shim from `src/mbgl/layout/symbol_instance.hpp` (no longer extending `std`).
- Updated all relevant signatures and uses from `std::source_location` to `mbgl::SourceLocation` in:
  - `src/mbgl/layout/symbol_instance.{hpp,cpp}`
  - `src/mbgl/renderer/bucket.hpp`
  - `src/mbgl/renderer/buckets/symbol_bucket.{hpp,cpp}`
- Updated build manifests:
  - `CMakeLists.txt`
  - `bazel/core.bzl`

**Verification**
- `cmake --build /app/build-linux-opengl --target mbgl-core` builds cleanly.
- `cmake --build /app/build-linux-opengl --target mbgl-test-runner` builds cleanly.
- Standalone compile check passed for both C++20 (standard path) and C++17 (fallback path).
- Full test run could not execute here because the environment has no display/EGL (`eglInitialize() failed`).

**Commit**
```
core: add standards-safe source_location fallback and support normal std path
954457e69b
```
